mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and SPX 20d forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or hit rate of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or rank IC is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and simultaneously rising consumer prices creates a financial conditions tightening that reduces equity valuations; when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increase month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Joint regression coefficient on the FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL interaction term is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the bivariate signal underperforms a univariate FEDFUNDS model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in housing starts presages a broader contraction in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship disappears in neutral or easing regimes.
failure_condition: In identified tightening regimes, HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60d SPX returns with rank IC below 0.04, or the signal performs equally well in easing regimes showing no regime asymmetry.

mechanism_narrative: A surprise drop in industrial production relative to recent trend causes a rapid repricing of near-term earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes coincide with positive 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll growth above the labor force growth trend indicates a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between PAYEMS 3-month change and SPX 60d returns breaks down with rolling 3-year rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls for two consecutive months, it confirms a manufacturing contraction that reduces cyclical earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Two-consecutive-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate reprices short-term discount rates abruptly, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity cash flows are discounted more heavily.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases coincide with positive 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
